rationale: Neither candidate captures the sole substantive ground-truth concern (test coverage for reduce_scatter, already resolved in the shown diff), so recall is near-zero for both. X's review is largely a confirmatory 'looks good, approve' report with one soft, vague suggestion and little concrete actionable content. Y surfaces three specific, diff-grounded nits with exact file:line citations and concrete fixes (stale comment describing outdated conditional logic, missing docstring, defensively dead code check) — all verifiable against the diff and genuinely useful to a reviewer, making it clearly more precise and actionable.
